task.objective: Enhance MCP authentication to detect tokens nearing expiration and include warning metadata in tool responses. This helps AI assistants and users proactively refresh tokens before they expire, preventing authentication failures.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Authentication
context.background:
MCP tokens have a 30-day validity period. Currently, when a token is close to expiration (e.g., 3 days remaining), users are not warned until the token actually expires. This leads to:
- Unexpected authentication failures during CI/CD workflows - User frustration when MCP tools suddenly stop working - Extra troubleshooting time to diagnose "token expired" errors
The JWT already contains an `exp` claim (set during token generation), but this information is not exposed to clients. The solution is to: 1. Extract expiration time during token validation 2. Include expiry metadata in MCPAuthContext 3. Add warning metadata to tool responses when token is near expiry
context.audience: - AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, etc.) - Developers using MCP tools programmatically - CI/CD pipelines with automated MCP integrations
context.impact: HIGH - Prevents authentication failures and improves user experience. Enables proactive token refresh before expiration.

existing_code.file: export interface MCPAuthContext { userId: number email: string organizationId: number organizationSlug: string orgRole: "owner" | "admin" | "viewer" userRole: "admin" | "viewer" }

functional.requirement: Extract token expiration from JWT payload during validation
Add tokenExpiresAt and tokenRefreshWarning to MCPAuthContext
Add warning metadata to responses when token expires within 7 days
Define constants for warning threshold (7 days) and critical threshold (3 days)
Include refresh command in warning message
Log warning on server when token is near expiry

non_functional.requirement: No performance impact - expiry check is O(1)
Backwards compatible - new fields are optional additions

solution.approach: 1. Extend MCPAuthContext interface with expiry fields 2. Modify validateMCPGeneratedToken to extract and calculate expiry info 3. Create helper function to check if refresh is needed 4. Optionally modify jsonResponse to include _meta warnings
solution.updated_interface:
// Constants for expiry thresholds const TOKEN_WARNING_DAYS = 7 // Start warning 7 days before expiry const TOKEN_CRITICAL_DAYS = 3 // Critical warning 3 days before expiry
export interface MCPAuthContext { userId: number email: string organizationId: number organizationSlug: string orgRole: "owner" | "admin" | "viewer" userRole: "admin" | "viewer" // NEW: Token expiry information tokenExpiresAt?: Date | null tokenExpiresInDays?: number | null tokenRefreshWarning?: 'none' | 'warning' | 'critical' | null }

verification.test: No dedicated unit tests exist for auth module.

steps.step: 2. Generate a new token via the settings page
3. Wait a few hours OR manually adjust the test to check 7-day threshold
4. Call any MCP tool with the token
5. Verify _warning field is present in response
6. Verify warning severity matches days remaining

test.alternative: For faster testing, temporarily modify the threshold constants: - Set TOKEN_WARNING_DAYS = 30 (always warn) - Test any MCP tool call - Verify warning appears - Reset constants

steps.step: 1. Observe warning in MCP tool response
4. Call MCP tool again with new token
5. Verify warning is gone (30 days remaining)

implementation_order.step: Extend MCPAuthContext interface with optional expiry fields
Modify validateMCPGeneratedToken to extract exp and calculate warning level
Modify jsonResponse or create wrapper to include warnings
Update all return statements in handleToolCall to include authContext

always.rule: Use underscore prefix for metadata fields (_warning) to distinguish from data
Include actionable refresh command in warning message

never.rule: Never block requests due to expiry warnings (only actual expiry blocks)
Never expose sensitive token data in warnings
Never change existing response structure (only add optional fields)

files_to_modify.file: lib/mcp/auth.ts - Add interface fields, modify validation, add helper

output_format.estimated_lines_changed: ~50 lines in auth.ts, ~30 lines in tools.ts
